title: Compare 2-digit numbers 2
description: Practice more challenging problems comparing numbers within 100.

assessment_items.question_content:
Gabe has $36$ marbles.  
Kia has $38$ marbles.

**Who has more marbles?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\pink3\blue6$ is $\pink3$ tens and $\blue6$ ones.

$\pink3\green8$ is $\pink3$ tens and $\green8$ ones.

Since both numbers have the same number of tens, let's look at the ones. 

$\green8$ ones is more than $\blue6$ ones.

$38$ is more than $36$.

Kia has more marbles than Gabe.

choices.content: Gabe and Kia  have the same number of marbles.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mrs. Garcia's class did $76$ push-ups.  
Mrs. Allen's class did $71$ push-ups.

**Which class did more push-ups?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\pink7\blue6$ is $\pink7$ tens and $\blue6$ ones.

$\pink7\blue1$ is $\pink7$ tens and $\blue1$ ones.

Since both numbers have the same number of tens, let's look at the ones. 

$\blue6$ ones is more than $\blue1$ one.

$76$ is more than $71$.

Mrs. Garcia's class did more push-ups than Mrs. Allen's class.

choices.content: Both classes did the same number of push-ups.

assessment_items.question_content:
On Saturday, you count $39$ stars.  
On Sunday, you count $93$ stars.  

**On which day did you count more stars?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\pink3\blue9$ is $\pink3$ tens and $\blue9$ ones.

$\pink9\blue3$ is $\pink9$ tens and $\blue3$ ones.

$\pink9$ tens is more than $\pink3$ tens.

$93$ is more than $39$.

You count more stars on Sunday.

choices.content: You count the same number of stars on Saturday and Sunday.

assessment_items.question_content:
The $\red{\text{red}}$ airplane has $83$ passengers.  
The $\blue{\text{blue}}$ airplane has $93$ passengers.  

**Which airplane has fewer passengers?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\pink8\blue3$ is $\pink8$ tens and $\blue3$ ones.

$\pink9\blue3$ is $\pink9$ tens and $\blue3$ one.


$\pink9$ tens is more than $\pink8$ tens.

$83$ is less than $93$.

The $\red{\text{red}}$ airplane has fewer passengers than the $\blue{\text{blue}}$ airplane.

choices.content: The $\red{\text{red}}$ airplane and the $\blue{\text{blue}}$ airplane have the same number of passengers.

assessment_items.question_content:
A mother bear collected $42$ berries.   
Her baby bear collected $44$ berries.  

**Who collected fewer berries?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\pink4\blue2$ is $\pink4$ tens and $\blue2$ ones.

$\pink4\blue4$ is $\pink4$ tens and $\blue4$ ones.

Since both numbers have the same number of tens, let's look at the ones. 

$\blue4$ ones is more than $\blue2$ ones.

$42$ is less than $44$.

The mother bear collected fewer berries than the baby bear.

choices.content: They both collected the same number of berries.

assessment_items.question_content:
Allison has $49$ books.   
Kris has $29$ books.

**Who has more books?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\pink4\blue9$ is $\pink4$ tens and $\blue9$ ones.

$\pink2\blue9$ is $\pink2$ tens and $\blue9$ ones.

$\pink4$ tens is more than $\pink2$ tens.

$49$ is more than $29$.

Allison has more books than Kris.

choices.content: Allison and Kris have the same number of books.

assessment_items.question_content:
On Monday, Santa received $65$ letters.   
On Tuesday, Santa received another $65$ letters.  

**On which day did Santa get more letters?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\pink6\blue5$ is $\pink6$ tens and $\blue5$ ones.
Santa got the same number of letters on Monday as on Tuesday.

choices.content: Santa got the same number of letters on Monday as on Tuesday.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Is each number less than $70$ or greater than $70$?**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
$\blue80$ is $\blue8$ tens, and $\green70$ is $\green7$ tens.

$\blue8$ tens are more than $\green7$ tens, so  **$80$ is greater than $70$**.
$5$ ones do not make any tens, so **$5$ is less than $70$.**
$5$ tens and $7$ ones are less than $\green7$ tens, so **$57$ is less than $70$.**
$7$ tens and $2$ ones are more than $\green7$ tens and $0$ ones,  so **$72$ is greater than $70$.**
$6$ tens and $8$ ones are less than $\green7$ tens, so **$68$ is less than $70$.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Is each number less than $30$ or greater than $30$?**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
$\blue40$ is $\blue4$ tens, and $\green30$ is $\green3$ tens.

$\blue4$ tens are more than $\green3$ tens, so  **$40$ is greater than $30$**.
$4$ ones do not make a ten, so **$4$ is less than $30$.**
$1$ ten and $3$ ones are less than $\green3$ tens, so **$13$ is less than $30$.**
$3$ tens and $1$ one are more than $\green3$ tens, so **$31$ is greater than $30$.**
$2$ tens and $7$ ones are less than $\green3$ tens, so **$27$ is less than $30$.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Is each number less than $87$ or greater than $87$?**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
$\blue90$ is $\blue9$ tens, and $\green80$ is $\green8$ tens.

$\blue9$ tens are more than $\green8$ tens, so  **$97$ is greater than $87$**.
$8$ ones do not make a ten, so **$8$ is less than $87$.**
$7$ tens and $7$ ones are less than $\green8$ tens and $7$ ones, so **$77$ is less than $87$.**
$8$ tens and $8$ ones are more than $\green8$ tens and $7$ ones, so **$88$ is greater than $87$.**
$8$ tens and $5$ ones are less than $\green8$ tens and $7$ ones, so **$85$ is less than $87$.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Is each number less than $50$ or greater than $50$?**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
$\blue70$ is $\blue7$ tens, and $\green50$ is $\green5$ tens.

$\blue7$ tens are more than $\green5$ tens, so  **$70$ is greater than $50$**.
$8$ ones do not make a ten, so **$8$ is less than $50$.**
$2$ tens and $1$ one are less than $\green5$ tens, so **$21$ is less than $50$.**
$8$ tens and $3$ ones are more than $\green5$ tens, so **$83$ is greater than $50$.**
$4$ tens and $9$ ones are less than $\green5$ tens, so **$49$ is less than $50$.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Is each number less than $42$ or greater than $42$?**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
$\blue52$ is $\blue5$ tens and $2$ ones, and $\green42$ is $\green4$ tens and $2$ ones.

$\blue5$ tens are more than $\green4$ tens, so  **$52$ is greater than $42$**.
$4$ ones do not make a ten, so **$4$ is less than $42$.**
$3$ tens and $2$ ones are less than $\green4$ tens, so **$32$ is less than $42$.**
$4$ tens and $3$ ones are more than $\green4$ tens and $2$ ones, so **$43$ is greater than $42$.**
$4$ tens and $1$ one are less than $\green4$ tens and $2$ ones, so **$41$ is less than $42$.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Is each number less than $25$ or greater than $25$?**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
$\blue31$ is $\blue3$ tens and $1$ one, and $\green25$ is $\green2$ tens and $5$ ones.

$\blue3$ tens are more than $\green2$ tens, so  **$31$ is greater than $25$**.
$9$ ones do not make a ten, so **$9$ is less than $25$.**
$1$ ten and $8$ ones are less than $\green2$ tens so **$18$ is less than $25$.**

$\green{2}$ tens and $\green{2}$ tens are the same.   So, we need to look at the ones.  

$4$ ones is less than $5$ ones.
 **$24$ is less than $25$.**

$9$ ones is more than $5$ ones.
**$29$ is greater than $25$.  **

assessment_items.question_content:
**Is each number less than $75$ or greater than $75$?**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
$\blue80$ is $\blue8$ tens and $0$ ones.  $\green75$ is $\green7$ tens and $5$ ones.

$\blue8$ tens are more than $\green7$ tens, so  **$80$ is greater than $75$**.
$7$ ones do not make a ten, so **$7$ is less than $75$.**
$6$ tens and $5$ ones are less than $\green7$ tens, so **$65$ is less than $75$.**
$7$ tens and $6$ ones are more than $\green7$ tens and $5$ ones, so **$76$ is greater than $75$.**
$7$ tens and $1$ one are less than $\green7$ tens and $5$ ones, so **$71$ is less than $75$.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Order the following numbers from least to greatest.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints:
$\blue5\pink2$ has $\blue5$ tens and $\pink2$ ones.

$\blue{5}\pink{7}$ has $\blue5$ tens and $\pink7$ ones.

$\blue6\pink3$ has $\blue6$ tens and $\pink3$ ones.  

$\blue6\pink8$ has $\blue6$ tens and $\pink8$ ones.

The least number is $52$ because it has $5$ tens and $2$ ones.

The greatest number is $68$ because it has $6$ tens and $8$ ones.
Here are the numbers in order from least to greatest: $52$, $57$, $63$, $68$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Order the following numbers from least to greatest.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints:
$\blue2\pink2$ has $\blue2$ tens and $\pink2$ ones.

$\blue{4}\pink{9}$ has $\blue4$ tens and $\pink9$ ones.

$\blue8\pink2$ has $\blue8$ tens and $\pink2$ ones.  

$\blue9\pink5$ has $\blue9$ tens and $\pink5$ ones.

The least number is $22$ because it has $2$ tens and $2$ ones.

The greatest number is $95$ because it has $9$ tens and $5$ ones.
Here are the numbers in order from least to greatest: $22$, $49$, $82$, $95$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Order the following numbers from least to greatest.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints:
$\blue7\pink0$ has $\blue7$ tens and $\pink0$ ones.

$\blue7\pink7$ has $\blue7$ tens and $\pink7$ ones.  

$\blue8\pink7$ has $\blue8$ tens and $\pink7$ ones.

$\blue8\pink8$ has $\blue8$ tens and $\pink8$ ones.

The least number is $70$ because it has $7$ tens and $0$ ones.

The greatest number is $88$ because it has $8$ tens and $8$ ones.
Here are the numbers in order from least to greatest: $70$, $77$, $87$, $88$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Order the following numbers from least to greatest.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints:
$\blue3\pink6$ has $\blue3$ tens and $\pink6$ ones.

$\blue6\pink3$ has $\blue6$ tens and $\pink3$ ones.  

$\blue6\pink4$ has $\blue6$ tens and $\pink4$ ones.

$\blue9\pink3$ has $\blue9$ tens and $\pink3$ ones.

The least number is $36$ because it has $3$ tens and $6$ ones.

The greatest number is $93$ because it has $9$ tens and $3$ ones.
Here are the numbers in order from least to greatest: $36$, $63$, $64$, $93$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Order the following numbers from least to greatest.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints:
$\pink8$ has $\blue0$ tens and $\pink8$ ones.

$\blue2\pink5$ has $\blue2$ tens and $\pink5$ ones.  

$\blue7\pink6$ has $\blue7$ tens and $\pink6$ ones.

$\blue9\pink9$ has $\blue9$ tens and $\pink9$ ones.

The least number is $8$ because it has no tens.

The greatest number is $99$ because it has $9$ tens and $9$ ones.
Here are the numbers in order from least to greatest: $8$, $25$, $76$, $99$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Order the following numbers from least to greatest.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints:
$\blue1\pink2$ has $\blue1$ ten and $\pink2$ ones.

$\blue{2}\pink{9}$ has $\blue2$ tens and $\pink9$ ones.

$\blue4\pink2$ has $\blue4$ tens and $\pink2$ ones.  

$\blue6\pink1$ has $\blue6$ tens and $\pink1$ ones.

The least number is $12$ because it has $1$ ten and $2$ ones.

The greatest number is $61$ because it has $6$ tens and $1$ ones.
Here are the numbers in order from least to greatest: $12$, $29$, $42$, $61$.
